title: Cannot reach rootless container port from remote host
body:
### Issue Description

I tried to access a rootless container's http server from a remote host.

I followed the instructions provided here: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/29f4572f9fe5d2f4c60821c34f867e99447d9102/docs/tutorials/basic_networking.md

But as an end result, I am not able to reach the container.
On the container host everything looks fine:
```
[rocky@diplodocus ~]$ ip a show eno1
2: eno1: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc mq state UP group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 58:47:ca:70:d0:74 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    altname enp1s0
    inet 192.168.1.52/24 brd 192.168.1.255 scope global dynamic noprefixroute eno1
       valid_lft 6277sec preferred_lft 6277sec
    inet6 fe80::5a47:caff:fe70:d074/64 scope link noprefixroute 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
[rocky@diplodocus ~]$ curl 192.168.1.52:8081
   ___          __              
  / _ \___  ___/ /_ _  ___ ____ 
 / ___/ _ \/ _  /  ' \/ _ `/ _ \
/_/   \___/\_,_/_/_/_/\_,_/_//_/

[rocky@diplodocus ~]$ curl 0.0.0.0:8081
   ___          __              
  / _ \___  ___/ /_ _  ___ ____ 
 / ___/ _ \/ _  /  ' \/ _ `/ _ \
/_/   \___/\_,_/_/_/_/\_,_/_//_/

```
But on the remote host (a laptop in the same network, at home, no firewall in the way)
```
guillaume@pavilion:~$ ping 192.168.1.52 -c 1
PING 192.168.1.52 (192.168.1.52) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 192.168.1.52: icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=2.22 ms

--- 192.168.1.52 ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 received, 0% packet loss, time 0ms
rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 2.218/2.218/2.218/0.000 ms
guillaume@pavilion:~$ curl 192.168.1.52:8081
curl: (7) Failed to connect to 192.168.1.52 port 8081 after 1 ms: No route to host
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
```
$ sudo podman system reset --force
```
```
$ podman network create podman1
```
```
$ podman run -dt --name webserver --net podman1 -p 8081:80 quay.io/libpod/banner
269fd0d6b2c8ed60f2ca41d7beceec2471d72fb9a33aa8ca45b81dc9a0abbb12
```

### Describe the results you received

The port 8081 on the host is closed when I try to access it from another host.

### Describe the results you expected

I should be able to reach that port, and see the beautiful `Podman` ascii art displayed to my amazed eyes.

### podman info output

```yaml
[rocky@diplodocus ~]$ podman info
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.29.0
  cgroupControllers:
  - memory
  - pids
  cgroupManager: systemd

  conmon:

    version: 'conmon version 2.1.7, commit: e6cdc9a4d6319e039efa13e532c1e58b713c904d'
  cpuUtilization:
    idlePercent: 100

    userPercent: 0
  cpus: 16
  distribution:

  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: diplodocus
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000

      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.14.0-284.25.1.el9_2.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  logDriver: journald
  memFree: 13139935232
  memTotal: 14117146624
  networkBackend: netavark
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-1.8.4-1.el9_2.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 1.8.4
      commit: 5a8fa99a5e41facba2eda4af12fa26313918805b
      rundir: /run/user/1000/crun
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: true
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:

      libseccomp: 2.5.2
  swapFree: 7256141824

```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Are you able to reach ports for non-containerized applications?

Hi @ashley-cui,
I can reach the port if I start a "normal" (non-rootless) container.
```
[rocky@diplodocus ~]$ sudo podman run -dt --name webserver --net podman -p 8082:80 quay.io/libpod/banner
98ca9060e5fff2ffcd6feff70166a310f90dcda08756a7e840b7950d8bc44101
```
```
guillaume@pavilion:~$ curl 192.168.1.52:8082
   ___          __              
  / _ \___  ___/ /_ _  ___ ____ 
 / ___/ _ \/ _  /  ' \/ _ `/ _ \
/_/   \___/\_,_/_/_/_/\_,_/_//_/

```

Does this answer your question?

Quick update: it might indeed be something wrong with my host.  If I open a port with netcat on the podman host, my remote host can not reach it (but in the other direction it works).

Any idea what that might be?  I guess it can be unrelated to podman

More updates: I figured out that if I "open" the port on my host using `firewall-cmd` (which I need to run as root :disappointed: ) the traffic goes through.  I did not expect that, it is pretty clear now that I should figure out a bit more about the host I am running podman on.  I can close this issue, I guess this is out of podman's scope of action.

Thanks for the heads up @ashley-cui !
